The AI race isn’t just about GPUs anymore. It’s about talent. The country that attracts and keeps the best researchers will shape the future of AI.

@aipost 🏴

🤩  AI helps fix OLED blue pixel efficiency

Researchers from Nagoya and Kyushu universities used AI to design new blue-emitting molecules for OLEDs. They screened over 19,000 carbon-based molecules with machine learning trained on quantum chemistry data from 1,000 samples.

The team synthesized two top candidates that achieved nearly 100% luminescence efficiency in films and produced pure blue light matching the Rec. 2020 standard. One molecule reached an external quantum efficiency of 35.2%.

This method bypasses the usual chemical complexity and degradation issues of blue OLED pixels. It opens a path to improving other materials with AI-driven molecular design.

📊@tech

🇨🇳 China just hit a new AI milestone.

RedNote, the company behind Xiaohongshu, says its new AI model dots-note-3.0 became the first to score a perfect 42/42 on the International Mathematical Olympiad.

That matters because the IMO isn’t multiple choice. Every solution requires a complete written proof, and human judges inspect every line. Missing a single logical step costs points.

Unlike most AI math benchmarks that only check the final answer, this model generated, tested, broke, and refined its own proofs using an agentic loop with Python before submitting them.

Even more impressive? The winning model is the smallest in the dots3 family.

Neuralink patients controlling their wheelchairs just by thinking. 

@aipost 🏴

🤖 OpenAI Faces Lawsuit Over ChatGPT Medical Advice

A US pastor sued OpenAI and Sam Altman after ChatGPT allegedly downplayed his lung embolism symptoms and discouraged him from seeking medical help. The lawsuit calls this "illegal medical practice" and negligence, citing the bot gave its own diagnoses and treatment plans while urging the man to ignore family warnings.

ChatGPT is not designed for medical diagnosis but reportedly handles 230 million medical queries weekly. The lawsuit also targets ChatGPT Health, a service where users upload medical documents, demanding it be halted until independently verified safe.

📊@tech
